Prerequisites: Azure CLI authenticated (az login already cached in the sandbox)
Docker daemon running and accessible via /var/run/docker.sock
Node.js, Python 3.11, .NET SDK, Java, and Docker available

Steps.Description: Relocate node_modules for all package.json folders to a repo-local directory to avoid NOEXEC issues (prevent host shell variable expansion)
Steps.Command: bash -lc 'set -e; base=/workspace/.nm-relocated; mkdir -p "$base"; find /workspace -maxdepth 5 -name package.json -not -path "*/node_modules/*" -not -path "*/.git/*" | while read -r pj; do d=$(dirname "$pj"); slug=$(echo "$d" | sed "s|^/workspace/?||; s|/|_|g"); if [ -z "$slug" ]; then slug=root; fi; tgt="$base/nm-$slug"; mkdir -p "$tgt"; rm -rf "$d/node_modules"; ln -sfn "$tgt" "$d/node_modules"; echo "  -> $d/node_modules -> $tgt"; done'

Steps.Description: Create a new Azure Developer CLI environment
Set the Azure subscription for the azd environment
Steps.Command: bash -lc "azd env set AZURE_SUBSCRIPTION_ID $(az account show --query id -o tsv)"

Steps.Description: Set the Azure tenant for the azd environment
Steps.Command: bash -lc "azd env set AZURE_TENANT_ID $(az account show --query tenantId -o tsv)"

Steps.Description: Reduce Azure OpenAI deployment capacity to fit available quota
Switch OpenAI chat model to gpt-4o-mini to avoid exhausted gpt-5-mini quota
Update Azure OpenAI gpt-4o-mini model version to a version supported in eastus
Remove relocated node_modules symlinks so Docker COPY does not conflict with container-installed node_modules
Steps.Command: bash -lc "find /workspace -type l -name node_modules -exec rm -f {} +; find /workspace -type d -name node_modules -prune -exec rm -rf {} + || true"

Steps.Description: Build ui-angular image in Azure Container Registry (avoid local docker build hang)
Steps.Command: bash -lc "cd /workspace/packages/ui-angular && az acr build --registry $(azd env get-values | grep AZURE_CONTAINER_REGISTRY_NAME | cut -d= -f2 | tr -d '\"') --image ui-angular:azd-$(date +%s) --file Dockerfile . --no-logs"

Steps.Description: Skip manual Container App update because ACR name and Container App are not available until after azd up provisions resources
Steps.Command: bash -lc "echo 'Skipping ui-angular containerapp update: resources will be created and deployed by azd up in the next step.'"

Steps.Description: Build ui-angular image remotely in Azure Container Registry to avoid local Docker build hang
Steps.Command: bash -lc "cd /workspace/packages/ui-angular && az acr build --registry $(azd env get-values | grep AZURE_CONTAINER_REGISTRY_NAME | cut -d= -f2 | tr -d '\"') --image ui-angular:azd-$(date +%s) --file Dockerfile . --no-logs"

Steps.Description: Update the existing ui-angular Container App to the newly built image
Steps.Command: bash -lc "ACR_NAME=$(azd env get-values | grep AZURE_CONTAINER_REGISTRY_NAME | cut -d= -f2 | tr -d '\"'); RG=$(azd env get-values | grep AZURE_RESOURCE_GROUP | cut -d= -f2 | tr -d '\"'); TAG=$(az acr repository show-tags -n $ACR_NAME --repository ui-angular --orderby time_desc --top 1 -o tsv); LOGIN=$(az acr show -n $ACR_NAME --query loginServer -o tsv); az containerapp update -n ui-angular -g $RG --image $LOGIN/ui-angular:$TAG"

Steps.Description: Mark ui-angular as already deployed so azd up skips the local docker build that previously stalled
Provision Azure resources and deploy all services using Azure Developer CLI
Ensure container images are built and pushed (azd deploy – idempotent verification after azd up)
